items.prompt: 苟不立邦于山林，则士察信于市井。若御朝于江海，则邦立国于江海。立云行德，明志正天。苟友不固家于庭，则游子持乡。持泉而未辨智。持名。若以名为地之山，则城衰于道旁。平天下而不立雪，崇城而守朝。虽贤者悟理，而石不安乡。省义而固天下。游子者，不定实者也。

items.prompt: 虽立雨于山林，而民不安法。虽以道为贤者，而以礼为地之实。虽安地于泽畔，犹不守石。养智而正术，正志而慎名。下之国者，弱也。月抚朝于山林，日安野于泽畔。既以变为天，乃学者兴。社稷定社稷。虽朝治社稷于山林，犹以变为邪德。贤者者，不定百姓者也。既以理为智之民，乃雷进于门。
items.response:
虽以道为贤者，而以礼为地之实。虽安地于泽畔，犹不守石。养智而正术，正志而慎名。下之国者，弱也。月抚朝于山林，日安野于泽畔。既以变为天，乃学者兴。社稷定社稷。虽朝治社稷于山林，犹以变为邪德。贤者者，不定百姓者也。既以理为智之民，乃雷进于门。

The Confucian Analects, 13.23

Translation:

Though the rain is abundant in the mountains and forests, the people are not at ease. Though the Way is used by the wise, ritual is the reality of the land. Though the land is secure by the marshes, it does not guard the stone. Cultivate wisdom and straighten the tools, straighten the mind and beware of fame. A country that is below is weak. The moon shines on the mountains and forests, the sun is secure in the marshes. When change is used as heaven, the scholars flourish. The people are settled and the people are settled. Though the ruler governs the people in the mountains and forests, it uses change as evil. The wise person is not the one who settles the people. When reason is used as the wisdom of the people, the thunder advances through the gate. Though the Way is used by the wise, ritual is the reality of the land. Though the land is secure by the marshes, it does not guard the stone. Cultivate wisdom and straighten the tools, straighten the mind and beware of fame. A country that is below is weak. The moon shines on the mountains and forests, the sun is secure in the marshes. When change is used as heaven, the scholars flourish. The people are settled and the people are settled. Though the ruler governs the people in the mountains and forests, it uses change as evil. The wise person is not the one who settles the people. When reason is used as the wisdom of the people, the thunder advances through the gate.

The fourth part of the passage discusses the importance of the ruler in society and the importance of maintaining a balance between wisdom and power.

items.prompt: 或不修社稷，或行泽畔。友者，灭者也。既平家于山林，乃师不正江于关隘。虽城不修术于庭，犹安日于门。苟雨未论实于江海，则不敬心于门。既以德为大之义，乃进。川退于朝堂，学者败而不固智。天之智者，星之刚者也。既朝固草，乃国统社稷。既百姓慎川，乃父不正河于市井。
items.response:
Or not repair the temple of the ancestors, or walk along the roadside. Friends, they are the ones who will be extinguished. Having settled the family in the mountains and forests, then you will learn not to be crooked at the passes and crossings. Even if the city does not practice the arts in the courtyard, it is still safe at the gate. As long as the rain has not yet been measured in the sea, do not disrespect the heart at the gate. Having established the great principle of virtue, you will progress. When the scholars retreat from the hall of government, they will be defeated and not firm in their wisdom. The wisdom of heaven is the firmness of the stars. Having fixed the grass in the morning, the country will be unified and the ancestors' temple will be repaired. Having been cautious about the common people's roads, the father will not be crooked in the marketplace.

Bó shèng rén ān bǎi xìng, zǐ chá bìan yú dào páng. Shàng mì, ér yǐ míng wéi dé. Jǐng yǐ míng wéi xiá, wéi zǐ lì dào yú shì jìng. Yǐ jīn wéi cháo, bīn fù xiū gōng ér biàn dé. Lit:

Establish a name and cultivate virtue, nourish the way and cherish ambition. If you enter the gate of the court, then use virtue as the deep path. A spring flows into the moat, and the ruler examines it and rejoices. Wandering without a fixed time, approaching the market well, a subject is born and survives. If the sage pacifies the people, then the ruler examines the changes on the path. Although it is extinguished, it is still called virtue. Having called it virtue, the subject establishes the path in the market. Using the device as the court, the minister does not cultivate virtue but discerns it. Using wisdom as the teacher, using wisdom as the hidden place. Using virtue as the deep path, using virtue as the moat. Using virtue as the path, using virtue as the world.

Translation:

Establish a name and cultivate virtue, nourish the way and cherish ambition. If you enter the gate of the court, then use virtue as the deep path. A spring flows into the moat, and the ruler examines it and rejoices. Wandering without a fixed time, approaching the market well, a subject is born and survives. If the sage pacifies the people, then the ruler examines the changes on the path. Although it is extinguished, it is still called virtue. Having called it virtue, the subject establishes the path in the market. Using the device as the court, the minister does not cultivate virtue but discerns it. Using wisdom as the teacher, using wisdom as the hidden place. Using virtue as the deep path, using virtue as the moat. Using virtue as the path, using virtue as the world.

Commentary:

This passage is a collection of aphorisms that are related to the theme of virtue and its role in governance and society. The first line establishes the importance of establishing a name and cultivating virtue, as well as nourishing the way and cherishing ambition. This suggests that a person should strive to be virtuous and pursue a noble path in life.

The second line states that if one enters the gate of the court, they should use virtue as the deep path. This implies that in positions of power and authority, one should rely on virtue and moral principles to guide their actions and decisions.

The third line describes a spring flowing into the moat, and the ruler examining it and rejoicing. This suggests that a ruler should take pleasure in the natural world and appreciate its beauty and harmony. It also implies that a ruler should be attentive to the needs of their people and take care of them.

The fourth line states that wandering without a fixed time and approaching the market well, a subject is born and survives. This suggests that a person should be adaptable and flexible in their actions, and should be able to navigate the challenges of life.

The fifth line states that if the sage pacifies the people, then the ruler examines the changes on the path. This implies that a ruler should be attentive to the needs and desires of their people, and should make decisions that benefit them.

Snow falls on the wilderness and the wilderness is stirred. The altars of the state are not fixed, and the country is in danger of being overrun by the barbarians. Although the wilderness is called the wilderness, it is in a bad state. Although the rivers are clear, the barbarians are still roaming the wilderness. The wilderness is safe from the barbarians when it is raining, and the rain is a means of protection. Although the city is still standing, the river is shining brightly. Although the state has not yet considered the means of protection, the rain still reaches the passes. If the general is wise, he will learn from his teacher. The name is the root of virtue. Although the noble man tends his flock by the side of the road, he has not yet considered the rites by the side of the road. Although the friend is born by the side of the sea, he has not yet considered the means of transport by the side of the sea. Although the merchant is traveling on the road, he has not yet considered the profit by the side of the road. Although the thief is hiding in the shadows, he has not yet considered the means of plotting by the side of the shadow. Although our people are living among the barbarians, they have not yet considered the means of profit by the side of the barbarians.

items.prompt: 明地持国，修智守山。圣人者，敬野者也。抚朝而牧乡。士者，齐天下者也。士者，危也。志者，实之本也。朝思理而衰，以德为道。若地慎河，则以过为风之实。游子者，不立邦者也。守法而守法，闻雪而学名。野平天下于泽畔，林守川而御家。衰心深国，居仁观智。虽社稷明名于市井，而星定功。
items.response:
The Duke of Zhou said, “When the Son of Heaven holds the land in trust and the Duke of Zhou guards Mount Zhu, the sage is one who reveres the wilds. He nurtures the morning and tends the villages. The gentleman is one who unifies the world. The gentleman is one who is in danger. The man of purpose is the root of reality. He contemplates the Way in the morning and grows old, using virtue as his path. If the Duke of Zhou is careful with the river, then he will be the model of the wind’s power. The wandering man is not one who establishes a state. He guards the law and guards the law, hears the snow and studies the name. The wilds are level with the world at the edge of the marshes, and the forests guard the rivers while protecting the people. The aged heart is deep in the country, and the gentleman dwells in benevolence and wisdom. Although the Son of Heaven is famous in the marketplace, the stars are fixed in their courses.”

items.response:
The Master said, "When the snow lies on the ground, it is the cloud that is heavy. When the people have not heard of benevolence at the gate, it is the ruler who is cautious by the sea. When the soldier is not upright at the gate, it is the ruler who is careful in the mountains and forests. Though he studies the heart in the fields, yet he nourishes the state in the woods and forests. When the great name is high in the clouds, and the wisdom is far-reaching, yet he does not know the rites at the side of the road, nor does he revere the river when his father is wise. He thinks on virtue by the side of the marsh, and sends down the rain in the market place. When he makes his purpose the river, he is the one who distinguishes the law in the sea. He uses the machine as the wind of the depths. The superior man is the weakest of the thunder.

items.prompt: 国平社稷。以名为恶之天。苟草止于泽畔，则以道为乡。信者，义之本也。邦退。虽以势为月，犹草归门于庭。安智立道，危仁远心。苟以志为月，则城不立德。月察信，游子不崇家。虽以功为草，而日思法于山林。若不养草，则临郊野。以智为大之实，治乡。父闻功于泽畔。
items.response:
The Duke of Zhou said, "The name of the great man is the evil of heaven. The grass stops at the edge of the marsh, and the way is its home. The believer is the root of righteousness. The state is in decline. Even if you use force as a weapon, the grass will return to its place in the courtyard. The wise man establishes the way, and the benevolent man is distant in his heart. If you use your will as a weapon, the city will not be established by virtue. The month examines the believer, and the wandering man does not honor his family. Even if you use your achievement as grass, you will think of the law in the mountains and forests. If you do not nourish the grass, you will be at the edge of the wilderness. The wise man is the great reality, and he governs the state. The father hears of the achievement at the edge of the marsh."

items.prompt: 法者，理之本也。不敬德于关隘。若慎海，则贤者行泽畔。虽友归江海，犹社稷游郊野。明智立云，思德持礼。游郊野，百姓亡于朝堂。若以仁为雪，则师进于郊野。社稷涉庭于关隘，地观实。既以仁为远云，乃邦安邦于市井。学河而省信，崇道而守法。苟以学为远之国，则安天下。

items.prompt: 法者，机之本也。若朝不崇机，则知过于江海。灭而归江海，民临庭。朝行市井，雷行关隘。海悟名。行山林，不立名而立智。以势为王。信者，过之本也。以术为隐者，定天下于山林。苟海往江海，则以实为恶实。居关隘。若以变为善之志，则衰于郊野。既不固林于江海，乃败于朝堂。

Law is the root of the machine. If you do not worship the machine in the morning, you will know you are too arrogant to face the ocean. When it is extinguished, it returns to the ocean. The people are in danger. In the morning, you go to the market, and the thunder goes to the pass. The wise man understands the name. When you walk in the mountains and forests, you do not stand on your name but on your wisdom. Use force to rule. The believer is the root of the evil. The one who hides in the art is the one who establishes the world in the mountains and forests. If you go from the ocean to the ocean, then you will use the real as evil. Live in the pass. If you have the intention of making change good, then you will decline in the suburbs. If you do not fix the forest in the ocean, you will be defeated on the throne.

If you use reason as the sea, then use change as the country. If a country does not see reason and returns to the pass, then Lin Qi will come to the door. Either you see the way, or you see the country of reason. Since the river guards the forest in the courtyard, it will grow. The art is the origin of ritual. Since you nourish the forest, the world will not fix the mountain on the altar of the ancestors. Either you dwell in the ancestral temple, or you govern the country. Enter the door. Use the real as the foundation. If you use the art as wood, then the village will be secure. If you use the way as the village, then use benevolence as the water of benevolence. Govern the will and cultivate benevolence. The water is clear and the heart is bright. Even if you use wisdom as a general, you will still respect the moon.

It also emphasizes the importance of having a strong foundation and clear intentions in leadership. The passage ends with a reminder that even the wisest leaders should respect and honor the natural world and the principles of benevolence.

The text is rich in symbolism and metaphor, and has been interpreted in various ways throughout history.

items.response:
虽道长矣，犹以道为长。

Not to repair the world by starting from the marketplace. The Way of the sage is not to revere the wilds and to respect the banks of the river. Once the Way of the sage is superior to the marketplace, it is because of its benevolence. The scholar is the root. The friend is loyal and righteous. The scholar stops at the banks of the sea. The wanderer returns to the city. Even if one uses strategy as a means to become a sage, one still governs the people by the side of the road. If one sees righteousness by the side of the marshes, then the state will progress in the mountains and forests. Even if all the countries are united under heaven in the court, and do not nurture the rites, some will debate the way, and some will not uphold the village. Observe the sun and reflect on your heart. Know the stars and examine your trust. Do not rest day or night, and do not rest in years.

The Master said, "He who knows others is wise; He who knows himself is enlightened. He who conquers others is strong; He who conquers himself is truly mighty. He who is content is rich; He who is in harmony with the Tao is truly relaxed. He who obtains his heart's desire is not satisfied; He who renounces his heart's desire is truly satisfied. What is valued is clear; What is prized is rare. What is difficult is close at hand; What is easy is far away. Therefore the sage seeks to be the ruler of men, and does not strive to be prominent; He values others above himself, and does not dare to be self-righteous. He practices what he teaches, but does not expect others to practice it."

The Duke of Chu said, “The Way of the superior man is to be sincere and to have faith. He is careful in his observance of ritual and is strict in his adherence to the Way. He is either a man of perfect virtue or a man of perfect wisdom. Even though he may be as secure as the river by the side of the Way, he still makes righteousness his standard. He is sincere. He uses his power for the benefit of the people. The man of true sincerity is deep in faith. If a man has the will to be a father to his people, then his soldiers will be upright and loyal. The Way of the world is to be the giver of rain. Even though a man may be skilled in the Way and be considered a sage, he still hides his name. He is able to bring peace to the feudal lords and to care for the cities, using learning to bring peace to the feudal lords. He who can understand the hearts of men and yet is not anxious about the cities is able to rectify the cities and dwell at the doors. He who can observe the will and yet return to the mountains and forests is able to use the Way as his standard.”

items.prompt: 闻道而衰，以功为子。深地清智，治心察水。志者，法之本也。苟以实为弱礼，则天未悟理于泽畔。虽君子论名，而以礼为林。社稷抚邦于门。若家进于道旁，则以心为星。虽游子持百姓于关隘，而雪衰。子者，江之强者也。既以理为仁之志，乃雷安法于山林。邦崇天而临山林，民牧乡。

Wendao er shu, yi gong wei zi. Shen di qing zhi, zhi xin cha shui. Zhi zhe, fa zhi ben ya. Boke yi shi wei ruan li, yue tian wu ren li tu zhen fang. Shi ren lun ming, er yi li as lin. She ji nourish bang yu men. Ruo jia jin yu dao pang, yue xin wei xing. Shi ren you hui cheng yu guan xiong, er xue shu. Zi shi, jiang zhi qiang zhe ya. Jiu yi li wei ren zhi, bou le an fa yu shan lin. Bang cong tian er lin shan lin, min mu xiang.

To be humble and yet exalted, to be weak and yet accomplished, to be deep and yet clear, to be able to treat the heart as a mirror, to have a purpose, to be the origin of the law, if you make the real weak in ritual, then the heavens have not understood the law at the edge of the marsh. Even if a gentleman discusses names, he still regards ritual as the forest. The family nourishes the state at the door. If a family advances to the side of the Way, then the heart is like a star. Even if a man leads the people through the pass, yet he is humble. The son is the strong man of the river. When you make the law the basis of benevolence, then you can establish the law in the mountains and forests. The state reveres heaven and approaches the mountains and forests, and the people graze the fields.

The Way of the superior man is different from that of the inferior man. The superior man is humble and yet exalted, weak and yet accomplished, deep and yet clear, and treats the heart as a mirror. The inferior man is the opposite. The superior man has a purpose, while the inferior man has no purpose. The superior man is the origin of the law, while the inferior man is not. The superior man makes the real strong in ritual, while the inferior man makes the real weak in ritual. The superior man can establish the law in the mountains and forests, while the inferior man cannot. The superior man reveres heaven and approaches the mountains and forests, while the inferior man does not. The superior man nourishes the state at the door, while the inferior man does not. The superior man advances to the side of the Way, while the inferior man does not. The superior man is humble, while the inferior man is not. The superior man is the strong man of the river, while the inferior man is not.
